non_deterministic_agent_testing.overview: Both agents use the same architecture but model decides whether to call tools non-deterministically

test_queries.query: What is the main purpose of the Direct Loan Program?

simple_agent.response_preview: The main purpose of the Direct Loan Program is for the U.S. Department of Education to make loans to help students and parents pay the cost of attendance (COA) at a postsecondary school.

helpfulness_agent.behavior: Model answered from its own knowledge, skipped tools, passed helpfulness check

key_findings.query_1: Helpfulness faster (3.19s vs 3.97s) - SKIPPED TOOLS (0 vs 1 call)
key_findings.query_2: Helpfulness faster (9.02s vs 11.65s) - BOTH used Tavily (1 call each)
key_findings.query_3: Helpfulness faster (4.37s vs 5.02s) - BOTH used Arxiv (1 call each)
key_findings.query_4: Helpfulness SLOWER (7.12s vs 5.56s) - BOTH used RAG (1 call each)

analysis.speed_pattern: Helpfulness agent faster in 3/4 queries, not always due to skipping tools
analysis.tool_usage: Both agents called tools in 3/4 queries - only Query 1 showed tool skipping by helpfulness agent
analysis.why_helpfulness_faster_when_using_tools: Helpfulness evaluation adds <1s overhead but may optimize token generation or use cached responses more efficiently
analysis.why_helpfulness_slower_query4: Extra helpfulness evaluation (5 messages vs 4) added overhead without compensating speedup
analysis.root_cause: Model's tool-calling decision is non-deterministic even at low temperature (0.1)
analysis.conclusion: Original hypothesis partially correct - tool skipping explains Query 1 speed advantage, but Queries 2-3 show helpfulness can be faster even WITH tools, possibly due to more concise generations or better caching
